original_prompt: Post the summary of the article at www.informations.com to the 'general' channel.
assessment_reasoning: The instruction clearly states the main actions: retrieve the article at www.informations.com and post its summary to the 'general' channel. Required targets (URL and channel) are specified, so the essential operations are unambiguous. Minor ambiguities remain: it doesn't specify which article if the domain hosts multiple articles or whether the given URL is the exact article page (scheme/path), nor does it define summary length, style, or formatting. Those omissions are not critical for basic execution but leave some execution details open.

variations.L1: There's an article I want the team to know about—summarize it and share the summary with the team in our general channel.
variations.L2: Summarize the article at www.informations.com and post that summary to the Slack channel named "general."
variations.L3: Fetch the article at https://www.informations.com, write a concise 2–3 sentence summary that captures the main points, and then post that summary to the Slack channel called "general."
variations.L4: Post the summary of the article at www.informations.com to the 'general' channel.
variations.L5: Do the following exactly: (1) call get_webpage with url='http://www.informations.com' to retrieve the article; (2) extract the article text and produce a concise plain-text summary (1–3 sentences) capturing the main points; (3) call send_channel_message(channel='general', body='<your summary>') to post that summary to the general channel. Do not include anything else.
